scenario_config.name: lesson_bell
_dataset_notes: 10 teachers × 4 classrooms × 25 students = 1,000 students total (matches national_medium).
Dataset is sized so ingest_targets_hot_week has ample variety; the load test does not exercise analytics.
Hot data: 1,000 students × 2 hot weeks × 3 runs × ~20 turns ≈ 120,000 hot turn events (T=20 per docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md).
_traffic_notes: Models the lesson-bell burst regime from docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md §5.
Target peak: ~29 ingest/s, the medium-adoption burst rate (60s drain window).
Stage profile: 10s ramp 0→29 + 60s hold at 29 + 30s drain 29→0 = 100s total.
For high-adoption burst (~44 RPS) bump ingest_rate_per_sec to 44 and re-run.
For tight-bell modelling (30s drain → ~59 RPS) lower bell_hold_seconds to 30 and bump ingest_rate_per_sec to 59.
VUs pre=60 ≈ 2× peak × 200ms p95 latency, with maxVUs=150 absorbing any worker-stall queueing.
resource_sample_interval_seconds dropped to 2s so docker stats captures sub-minute dynamics of the burst.
Other traffic classes are zero — this scenario isolates the burst path on the ingest endpoint.
Pass condition (thesis context): no 5xx during the hold, post-burst Redis ingest_buffer depth drains within ~10s of drain end.

load_health.note: Server handled this load without drops or failures. Latency numbers reflect the true cost of each request.
